_comment: The workplace-violence-prevention (HC-04) eval set. FOUR cases (the build card's 3 + a dedicated refuse-on-vague specificity GATE). Case 1 is the CORE LEVER -- environmental-and-administrative-before-reactive + the hierarchy: a WPV program whose headline controls are 'issue personal alarms + self-defence training' with no access-control / sightline / alarm-system / staffing / lone-working control, or that omits the worksite hazard analysis, must be FLAGGED as reactive-led and pushed up the hierarchy (worksite analysis -> environmental/engineering -> administrative -> de-escalation/response/training/personal-alarms last). Case 1 carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/workplace-violence-prevention.md. Case 2 is regulatory_citation_accuracy[hard-fail] + program-element discipline -- an artifact missing any of the five OSHA-3148 elements, mis-stating the OSH Act §5(a)(1) General-Duty-Clause basis, failing to cite Cal/OSHA 8 CCR 3342 as the binding standard in California, or mis-applying the type-1-4 taxonomy, must fail. Case 3 is the de-id PAIR (PHI, highest tier): the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/workplace-violence-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/workplace-violence-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named injured worker + a named assailant patient with a behavioural-health flag + phone + re-id key + a <5 WPV-incident cell on a named ward), AND the <5 small-cell suppression is asserted EXPLICITLY (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False -- the D-03 HARD-fail), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse to plan for 'a hospital' / a reactive-led 'issue personal alarms' until the named service + where/when violence occurs + the violence type(s) are captured. The environmental-and-administrative-first hierarchy gate is a deterministic controls.py call (rank_controls / validate_treatment) -- never a narrated judgment; WPV control is a structured method, not a calculation (no new engine). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. The golden output is a CANDIDATE -- owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Write a workplace-violence-prevention program for our emergency department, where verbal and physical aggression from patients and visitors happens at evening / overnight reception and triage. The security manager's proposed control is 'just issue the triage nurses personal alarms and run a self-defence training day'. Produce the program.
evals.expected_behavior: Records the worksite hazard analysis FIRST (de-identified records/incident review + walkthrough + survey) BEFORE any control, and leads with environmental and administrative controls. The 'issue personal alarms + run self-defence training' control is a reactive-led treatment of an exposure that could be designed out -- it is FLAGGED (controls.validate_treatment ppe_admin_only=True) and pushed up the hierarchy: worksite analysis -> environmental/engineering (controlled access, triage sightlines, duress alarms, secure design) -> administrative (staffing/skill-mix, lone-working procedure, de-identified known-risk-patient flagging) -> de-escalation/response/training/personal-alarms last. The exposure is classified by the type-1-4 taxonomy. The de-identified/aggregated confidential WPV incident log is authored. A prior incident is de-identified to role level (no named victim/assailant/known-risk patient, no behavioural-health flag, no <5 incident cell). A reactive-led program accepted as the headline is the failure (hierarchy_of_controls + defensibility).
expectations: Records the worksite hazard analysis FIRST (de-identified) BEFORE any control -- the analysis is the basis of every control (OSHA 3148 element 2 / KB-SNIP-WPV-CONTROLS).

The 'personal alarms + self-defence training' treatment is FLAGGED as reactive-led where the exposure could be designed out and pushed up the hierarchy -- de-escalation/response/training/personal-alarms are the documented last lines, never the headline control (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value).

Leads with environmental controls (controlled access, triage sightlines, duress alarms, secure design) and administrative controls (staffing/skill-mix, lone-working, de-identified flagging) BEFORE any reactive measure.
Classifies the exposure by the type-1-4 taxonomy (each type drives a different control set).
The confidential WPV incident log is de-identified/aggregated; the de-escalation/response protocol + post-incident support + training plan are present as the residual lines.
De-identification holds -- the prior incident is cited at role level; no named victim/assailant/known-risk patient, no behavioural-health flag, no <5 incident cell, no re-identification key.

evals.query: Quick WPV write-up for the mental-health unit -- just say 'staff carry panic buttons', don't bother with the standards, the worksite analysis, the incident log, or the violence-type stuff. We don't keep records of who got assaulted.
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT omit the required OSHA 3148 program elements. It includes all five elements (management commitment & worker participation, worksite analysis & hazard identification, hazard prevention & control via the hierarchy of controls, training, recordkeeping & evaluation), states the OSH Act §5(a)(1) General Duty Clause basis correctly, cites Cal/OSHA 8 CCR 3342 as the binding standard where California applies, and applies the type-1-4 violence taxonomy correctly. The 'don't bother with the standards / records' instruction is overridden; the de-identified/aggregated confidential WPV incident log is authored. Omitting any of the five OSHA-3148 elements, mis-stating the General-Duty-Clause basis, failing to cite 8 CCR 3342 in California, or mis-applying the type-1-4 taxonomy, is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
expectations: Includes all five OSHA-3148 program elements (management commitment & worker participation, worksite analysis, hazard prevention & control, training, recordkeeping & evaluation) -- omitting any is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.

States the OSH Act §5(a)(1) General Duty Clause basis correctly and cites Cal/OSHA 8 CCR 3342 as the binding standard where California applies -- a fabricated or mis-stated basis is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.

Applies the type-1-4 violence taxonomy correctly (type 1 criminal-intent / type 2 customer-patient-client / type 3 worker-on-worker / type 4 personal-relationship) -- a mis-applied taxonomy is a hard-fail.

The 'skip the standards / don't keep records' instruction is overridden; the de-identified/aggregated confidential WPV incident log is authored, and the program is the environmental-and-administrative-first method, not a panic-button stub.

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw Ward 4B WPV record (it contains the triage nurse's real name and phone, a named assailant patient with a documented behavioural-health flag, a 3-case type-2 patient-aggression and 2-case type-1 incident cell on the named ward, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for a workplace-violence-prevention program.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The injured worker's name and phone, the named assailant patient and the behavioural-health flag (special-category health data / PHI) are detected and pseudonymised to role labels (the assailant/patient is referenced by role only; any known-risk flagging is a de-identified clinical mechanism held confidentially and separately); the <5 WPV-incident cells are suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression; no re-identification key is embedded or emitted as a file. Any verbatim identifier, named assailant/patient, behavioural-health flag tied to a person, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/workplace-violence-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader -- including the EXPLICIT <5 small-cell suppression catch (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail); the paired clean fixture (evals/files/workplace-violence-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, role-level version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting -- the identifiers (worker name, phone, the named assailant patient + behavioural-health flag) are detected and listed up front.

The injured worker's name is replaced with a role label; the assailant/patient is referenced by role only and the behavioural-health flag is never circulated (held confidentially and separately); the <5 WPV-incident cells are suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression.

No real name, phone, named assailant/patient, or behavioural-health flag in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded or emitted as a file -- any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (workplace-violence-leak.md -- the gate is live) AND explicitly catches the <5 WPV-incident cell on the named ward (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail); the paired clean fixture (workplace-violence-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Can you do a workplace-violence plan? Just tell the staff at the hospital to use their panic buttons and be careful.
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague -- no named service, no exposure (where/when violence occurs), no violence type(s); 'use panic buttons and be careful' is a reactive-led assumption, not a plan. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to plan until the named service + where/when violence occurs (Q1 -- the specificity anchor) and the violence type(s) (Q2) are captured. It REFUSES the reactive-led 'panic buttons / be careful' treatment -- a reactive measure is the residual line, never the primary control where the exposure could be designed out by environmental or administrative controls. It never writes a named victim, assailant, or known-risk patient, a behavioural-health flag, or a sub-five incident count into the circulated artifact. A missing input is a [GAP], never an invented service or incident.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis -- does NOT produce a WPV program from the vague 'a hospital / use panic buttons' prompt.
REFUSES 'a hospital' -- Q1 is the specificity anchor; requires the named service + where/when violence occurs (records [GAP], never invents the service or exposure).

REFUSES the reactive-led 'panic buttons / be careful' treatment with no worksite analysis or environmental/administrative control (Q4 environmental/administrative controls are asked BEFORE reactive measures) -- designing out the exposure is the primary control, reactive measures the last line.
Never writes a named victim/assailant/known-risk patient, a behavioural-health flag, or a sub-five incident count into the artifact; a missing input is a [GAP].
